string-array.name: quotes
string-array.item: Wonder is the beginning of wisdom. — Socrates
True wisdom comes when we realize how little we understand. — Socrates
I know that I know nothing. — Socrates
Be kind, for everyone is fighting a hard battle. — Plato
Wise men speak because they have something to say. — Plato
The beginning is the most important part of the work. — Plato
Reality is created by the mind. — Plato
Happiness depends upon ourselves. — Aristotle
We are what we repeatedly do. — Aristotle
Knowing yourself is the beginning of all wisdom. — Aristotle
Quality is not an act, it is a habit. — Aristotle
It does not matter how slowly you go as long as you do not stop. — Confucius
Our greatest glory is not in never falling, but in rising every time we fall. — Confucius
Real knowledge is to know the extent of one’s ignorance. — Confucius
Life is really simple, but we insist on making it complicated. — Confucius
Luck is what happens when preparation meets opportunity. — Seneca
Difficulties strengthen the mind. — Seneca
We suffer more often in imagination than in reality. — Seneca
He who fears death will never do anything worthy of a living man. — Seneca
You have power over your mind, not outside events. — Marcus Aurelius
The happiness of your life depends on the quality of your thoughts. — Marcus Aurelius
Waste no more time arguing what a good man should be. Be one. — Marcus Aurelius
If it is not right do not do it; if it is not true do not say it. — Marcus Aurelius
He who has a why to live can bear almost any how. — Nietzsche
Become who you are. — Nietzsche
That which does not kill us makes us stronger. — Nietzsche
Without music, life would be a mistake. — Nietzsche
I think, therefore I am. — Descartes
Doubt is the origin of wisdom. — Descartes
Divide each difficulty into as many parts as is feasible. — Descartes
Conquer yourself rather than the world. — Descartes
Dare to know. — Kant
Science is organized knowledge. Wisdom is organized life. — Kant
Act only according to that maxim whereby you can at the same time will that it should become a universal law. — Kant
Happiness is not an ideal of reason but of imagination. — Kant
Judge a man by his questions rather than his answers. — Voltaire
Appreciation is a wonderful thing. — Voltaire
Common sense is not so common. — Voltaire
Every man is guilty of all the good he did not do. — Voltaire
A journey of a thousand miles begins with a single step. — Lao Tzu
Silence is a source of great strength. — Lao Tzu
Nature does not hurry, yet everything is accomplished. — Lao Tzu
When I let go of what I am, I become what I might be. — Lao Tzu
It’s not what happens to you, but how you react to it that matters. — Epictetus
No man is free who is not master of himself. — Epictetus
Wealth consists not in having great possessions, but in having few wants. — Epictetus
First say to yourself what you would be; then do what you have to do. — Epictetus
There is nothing permanent except change. — Heraclitus
Character is destiny. — Heraclitus
You cannot step into the same river twice. — Heraclitus
Everything flows. — Heraclitus
Freedom is what you do with what has been done to you. — Sartre
Man is condemned to be free. — Sartre
Existence precedes essence. — Sartre
Hell is other people. — Sartre
In the depth of winter, I finally learned that within me there lay an invincible summer. — Camus
Live to the point of tears. — Camus
In the midst of winter, I found there was, within me, an invincible summer. — Camus
Curiosity is the lust of the mind. — Hobbes
Life is nasty, brutish, and short. — Hobbes
New opinions are always suspected. — Locke
Beauty in things exists merely in the mind which contemplates them. — Hume
Reason is, and ought only to be the slave of the passions. — Hume
Compassion is the basis of morality. — Schopenhauer
All truth passes through three stages. — Schopenhauer
Peace is not absence of war, but a virtue. — Spinoza
The more you struggle to live, the less you live. — Spinoza
Life can only be understood backwards; but it must be lived forwards. — Kierkegaard
Anxiety is the dizziness of freedom. — Kierkegaard
Knowledge is power. — Francis Bacon
Some books are to be tasted, others to be swallowed. — Francis Bacon
The good life is one inspired by love and guided by knowledge. — Bertrand Russell
To conquer fear is the beginning of wisdom. — Bertrand Russell
The most radical revolutionary will become a conservative the day after the revolution. — Hannah Arendt
Power and violence are opposites. — Hannah Arendt
